summary: Backend-only testing of Spades Big Wheel ruleset + admin live-seats endpoint. All 15 new tests pass and 129/129 regression tests still green. BIG_WHEEL deck (54 cards), trick-winner ordering (Big Joker > Little Joker > 2♠ > 2♦ > A♠), ruleset preference persistence, admin/live-seats schema and empty-state behavior all verified.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/spades.py imports RULESETS twice (line 6 top-level + line 69 inline noqa). Minor DRY issue only — the inline import is redundant. Safe to remove.
admin_live_seats._hydrate_user is awaited in a tight per-seat loop; for high seat counts consider asyncio.gather() to parallelize user+profit_share lookups.

spades.play_card persists tricks_played & spades_broken via update but reconstructs them with game_doc.get(..., default) — safe, but legacy rows lacking these keys silently default to 0/False. Consider a one-time migration or an explicit default-on-insert to avoid drift.

user_preferences.set_spades_ruleset uses a 3-item SPADES_RULESETS list hard-coded instead of deriving from utils.spades_game.RULESETS. If a new ruleset is added later, this list will go stale — consider `list(RULESETS.keys())`.
test_credentials: Admin vault: GlobalVibez_Founder_2025! / code 000000. Standard users via POST /api/auth/demo-login.
seed_data_creation: Fixture creates 4 demo users and up to 4 BIG_WHEEL/CLASSIC spades_games. Teardown deletes all created game_ids. After run, verified `spades_games` collection has 0 docs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_spades_big_wheel_and_admin_seats.py is reusable for regression. It hits live preview URL via REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL and also imports utils.spades_game for unit-style deck/trick-winner assertions. Cleanup fixture uses pymongo directly so DB stays tidy. Tested: rulesets listing, start (BIG_WHEEL/CLASSIC/invalid), preference GET/PUT, admin/live-seats auth + empty-state + populated state (incl. spectate_url format /spades-aaa/spades_xxxxxx).
